Paris is one of the most well-known capital cities in the world, serving as France's political, cultural, and economic center. It is home to the French government, including the Élysée Palace (the official residence of the President) and the National Assembly.

Results.Query: Write an apology letter to a friend whose birthday I missed
Results.ActualAnswer:
<thinking>
The user wants help writing an apology letter to a friend whose birthday they missed. This is a creative writing request, not a factual query, so I don't need to use Wikipedia for this. I can directly write the letter.
</thinking>

Here's a heartfelt apology letter you can personalize for your friend:

---

**Subject: I'm So Sorry I Missed Your Birthday 🎂**

Dear [Friend's Name],

I hope this letter finds you well. I'm writing to sincerely apologize for missing your birthday. There is truly no excuse good enough for forgetting such an important day — a day that celebrates **you**, someone I deeply care about.

I know how much birthdays mean, and the thought that I wasn't there to make you feel special truly weighs on my heart. You have always shown up for me in ways that matter, and I failed to do the same for you. That wasn't fair, and you deserved better from me.

I want you to know that missing your birthday was never a reflection of how much you mean to me. You are one of the most [kind / funny / generous / wonderful — pick what fits!] people in my life, and I am incredibly lucky to call you my friend.

I'd love the chance to make it up to you. How about we plan a **belated birthday celebration** — just the two of us (or the whole crew!) — dinner, dessert, and all the fun you deserve? The celebration of *you* doesn't have to stop at just one day.

Once again, I am truly sorry. I value our friendship more than words can express, and I promise to do better.

With love and a big (overdue) **Happy Birthday** 🎉,

[Your Name]

---

### ✏️ Tips to Personalize This Letter:
- **Add a specific memory** you share with your friend to make it more genuine.
- **Mention the reason** (briefly and honestly) if it was circumstantial — but don't over-explain or make excuses.
- **Attach a gift or gift card** to go along with the letter for an extra thoughtful touch.
- **Handwrite it** if possible — a handwritten note always feels more sincere than a typed one.

The most important thing is that your friend feels seen, valued, and appreciated. Good luck! 💛

Results.Query: Who was the first president of the United States?
Results.ActualAnswer:
<thinking>
The search results clearly confirm that George Washington was the first president of the United States, serving from 1789 to 1797.
</thinking>

**[George Washington](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_Washington)** was the first President of the United States. He was a Founding Father who served two terms as president, from **1789 to 1797**. Before his presidency, he was also the commander of the Continental Army during the American Revolutionary War.

Results.Query: When was the Eiffel Tower built?
Results.ActualAnswer:
<thinking>
The search results clearly show that the Eiffel Tower was built from 1887 to 1889. Let me provide a well-cited answer.
</thinking>

The **Eiffel Tower** was constructed between **1887 and 1889**. It is named after the engineer **Gustave Eiffel**, whose company designed and built it. The tower was built as a centerpiece for the **1889 World's Fair (Exposition Universelle)**, held in Paris to celebrate the 100th anniversary of the Storming of the Bastille. [Eiffel Tower](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eiffel_Tower)

Upon its completion, it became the **world's tallest structure** at the time. [History of the world's tallest buildings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_the_world%27s_tallest_buildings)

---

### References
- [Eiffel Tower – Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eiffel_Tower)
- [History of the world's tallest buildings – Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_the_world%27s_tallest_buildings)
- [Exposition Universelle (1889) – Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Exposition_Universelle_(1889))

## Population of Tokyo

Tokyo, officially known as the **Tokyo Metropolis**, is the capital and most populous city of Japan. As of **2023**, Tokyo has a population of over **14 million people** in the city proper, with the figure recorded at approximately **14,195,730** — representing about **11.5% of Japan's total population**. [Tokyo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tokyo) [Demographics of Tokyo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Demographics_of_Tokyo)

Tokyo is not only the most populous city in Japan but also one of the most populous metropolitan areas in the entire world.

Results.Query: What year did World War II end?
Results.ActualAnswer:
<thinking>
The user is asking about when World War II ended. This is a simple, well-known historical fact that doesn't require a Wikipedia search. World War II ended in 1945.

World War II ended in **1945**. The war concluded in two stages:
- **May 8, 1945 (V-E Day):** Victory in Europe, marking the surrender of Nazi Germany.
- **September 2, 1945 (V-J Day):** Victory over Japan, after Japan's formal surrender, which ended the war in the Pacific.

Results.Query: Who invented the telephone?
Results.ActualAnswer:
<thinking>
The search results clearly point to Alexander Graham Bell as the person credited with patenting the first practical telephone. There are also mentions of Antonio Meucci and others. Let me provide a comprehensive answer based on these results.
</thinking>

## The Invention of the Telephone

The invention of the telephone is most commonly credited to **Alexander Graham Bell**, a Scottish-born inventor, scientist, and engineer who is credited with **patenting the first practical telephone**. He also co-founded the American Telephone and Telegraph Company (AT&T). [Alexander Graham Bell](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexander_Graham_Bell)

However, the history of the telephone's invention is more complex and involves multiple contributors:

- 🇮🇹 **Antonio Meucci**, an Italian inventor, is also recognized by many as a key figure in the telephone's development, with some crediting him as the true inventor of the device. [Antonio Meucci](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antonio_Meucci)
- 🇩🇪 **Philipp Reis** invented an early sound-transmitting device called the **Reis telephone** as far back as **1857**, with his first successful work dated to **October 1861**. [Reis telephone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reis_telephone)

The question of who truly "invented" the telephone has been the subject of significant legal and historical debate, involving lawyers, patents, and competing claims. [History of the Telephone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_the_telephone)

---

### References
- [Alexander Graham Bell – Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexander_Graham_Bell)
- [Antonio Meucci – Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antonio_Meucci)
- [Reis Telephone – Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reis_telephone)
- [History of the Telephone – Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_the_telephone)

Results.Query: What is the speed of light?
Results.ActualAnswer:
<thinking>
The speed of light is a simple, well-known scientific fact that doesn't require a Wikipedia search. I can answer this directly.
</thinking>

The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately **299,792,458 meters per second (m/s)**, which is roughly **3 × 10⁸ m/s** or about **186,282 miles per second**.

This value is denoted by the letter **c** and is a fundamental constant of nature. According to Einstein's theory of special relativity, nothing with mass can travel at or faster than the speed of light — making it the ultimate cosmic speed limit.

Results.Query: Who built the Space Needle?
Results.ActualAnswer:
<thinking>
I have some information from my searches. Let me now put together a comprehensive answer:
1. The Space Needle was built for the 1962 World's Fair in Seattle.
2. The Howard S. Wright Companies built the Space Needle (from the first search).
3. The architect/designer was John Graham (mentioned in the Architecture of Seattle article), and more recently (for a renovation) Olson Kundig Architects.

Let me search one more time specifically to confirm the original designer/architect of the Space Needle.
</thinking>

Based on the search results, here is a comprehensive answer:

The **Space Needle** was **designed** by architect **John Graham** and his firm, and it was **constructed by the Howard S. Wright Companies**. It was built for the **1962 World's Fair** (Century 21 Exposition) in Seattle, Washington, rising 605 feet (184 m) tall. [Space Needle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Space_Needle)

The construction was carried out by the [Howard S. Wright Companies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Howard_S._Wright_Companies), a Seattle-based construction firm. The original concept is widely credited to **Edward Carlson**, a Seattle businessman, who sketched the initial idea on a napkin, while **John Graham & Company** developed and finalized the architectural design. [Architecture of Seattle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Architecture_of_Seattle)

In more recent years, for its major renovation, the Space Needle was redesigned by **Olson Kundig Architects**, with **Hoffman Construction Company** serving as the general contractor. [Space Needle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Space_Needle)

---

### References
- [Space Needle – Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Space_Needle)
- [Howard S. Wright Companies – Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Howard_S._Wright_Companies)
- [Architecture of Seattle – Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Architecture_of_Seattle)
